title: in6_addr detection ifdef cascade is fragile
description:
ipv6_addr.h:43-62 has an ifdef cascade to detect if struct in6_addr is already defined:
```c
#if defined(LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR) && LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR
#elif defined(IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT)
#elif defined(CONFIG_NET_IPV6) && defined(__ZEPHYR__)
#elif defined(_NETINET_IN_H) || defined(_NETINET_IN_H_) || ...
#else
struct in6_addr { ... };
#endif
```

What goes wrong: New platforms with different guard macros will silently get the fallback definition, which may conflict with the system's struct in6_addr if it's later included, causing redefinition errors or subtle ABI mismatches.

The code already has LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR as an escape hatch, which is good. Consider making the fallback definition more defensive:
```c
#else
/* Fallback definition - if this conflicts with your platform's in6_addr,
 * define LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR=1 before including this header */
struct in6_addr {
    uint8_t s6_addr[16];
};
#endif
```

The existing comment at line 40-42 partially addresses this but could be more visible at the definition site.
